nodes.title: Longindying call
nodes.snippet: And a call, pure, long and throbbing. Longindying call. / a call came, long in dying. That was a tuningfork the tuner had that he forgot that he now struck. A call again. That he now poised that it now throbbed. You hear? It throbbed, pure, purer, softly and softlier, its buzzing prongs. Longer in dying call.

nodes.title: Tup
nodes.snippet: Full tup. Full throb. / Tipping her tepping her tapping her topping her. Tup. Pores to dilate dilating. Tup. The joy the feel the warm the. Tup. To pour o'er sluices pouring gushes. Flood, gush, flow, joygush, tupthrob.

nodes.title: Alluring
nodes.snippet: Ah, lure! Alluring. / Bloom wound a skein round four forkfingers, stretched it, relaxed, and wound it round his troubled double, fourfold, in octave, gyved them fast. / Bloom looped, unlooped, noded, disnoded.

nodes.title: Croppy Boy
nodes.snippet: Black. / Deepsounding. Do, Ben, do. / Low in dark middle earth. Embedded ore.

nodes.title: Leave it to my hands
nodes.snippet: Fro. To, fro. A baton cool protruding. / And leave it to my hands / On the smooth jutting beerpull laid Lydia hand, lightly, plumply, leave it to my hands. All lost in pity for croppy. Fro, to: to, fro: over the polished knob (she knows his eyes, my eyes, her eyes) her thumb and finger passed in pity: passed, repassed and, gently touching, then slid so smoothly, slowly down, a cool firm white enamel baton protruding through their sliding ring.

nodes.title: Ben Dollard
nodes.snippet: Big Benaben. Big Benben. / Ben Dollard's vague bass / Ben Dollard's voice. Base barreltone. Doing his level best to say it. Croak of vast manless moonless womoonless marsh. Other comedown. Big ships' chandler's business he did once. Remember: rosiny ropes, ships' lanterns. Failed to the tune of ten thousand pounds. Now in the Iveagh home. Cubicle number so and so. Number one Bass did that for him.
